The following code fails:

```python
john = mixer.blend(Actor, name='John')
cesar = mixer.blend(Actor, name='Cesar')

mixer.cycle(3).blend(Movie, discipline=(x for x in (cesar, cesar, john)))
```

Here is the traceback:

```
    def emit_backref_from_scalar_set_event(state, child, oldchild, initiator):
        if oldchild is child:
            return child
        if oldchild is not None and \
                oldchild is not PASSIVE_NO_RESULT and \
                oldchild is not NEVER_SET:
            # With lazy=None, there's no guarantee that the full collection is

issue_title: dict(FlatDict) fails when it contains complex unicode keys
issue_body:
There seem to be various issues when I put a complex unicode key inside the flatdict. 

For example:

```python
import flatcdict
d = flatdict.Flatdict()
d[u'key1:key2'] = 'value1'
dict(d)

KeyError: u'key1'
```

The same thing works if the key is a string object
```python
import flatcdict
d = flatdict.Flatdict()
d['key1:key2'] = 'value1'
dict(d)

{'key1:key2': 'value1'}
```
I tracked the issue to [this line](https://github.com/gmr/flatdict/blob/master/flatdict.py#L364) where you force the key to be an 'str' object for it to have a delimiter. 

Any special reason for that?

```python
def _has_delimiter(self, key):
    """Checks to see if the key contains the delimiter.

    :rtype: bool

    """
    return isinstance(key, str) and self._delimiter in key
```

Its a problem for me because I populate the flatdict with objects returned by the ```json.loads``` function, which uses **unicode** for keys.

**Environment**

Python 2.7.14 on macOS HighSierra 10.13.3

I'll be happy to contribute and fix this issue once we discuss it. 

Thanks 👍

The following code:
```python
import numpy as np
import pint
ureg = pint.UnitRegistry()
a = np.arange(3) * ureg.meter
np.insert(a, 2, 4 * ureg.meter)
```
produces:
```pytb
~/miniconda3/envs/py36/lib/python3.6/site-packages/numpy/lib/function_base.py in insert(arr, obj, values, axis)
   4439         new[tuple(slobj)] = arr[tuple(slobj2)]
   4440         if wrap:
-> 4441             return wrap(new)
   4442         return new
   4443     elif indices.size == 0 and not isinstance(obj, np.ndarray):

~/miniconda3/envs/py36/lib/python3.6/site-packages/pint/quantity.py in __array_wrap__(self, obj, context)
   1342 
   1343     def __array_wrap__(self, obj, context=None):
-> 1344         uf, objs, i_out = context
   1345 
   1346         # if this ufunc is not handled by Pint, pass it to the magnitude.

TypeError: 'NoneType' object is not iterable
```

I have no idea if `insert` has any hope of working right now, but that exception comes from code that is obviously wrong:

https://github.com/hgrecco/pint/blob/241bf7df1404e26d202df64e12e772d111803c31/pint/quantity.py#L1428-L1430

Here `context` defaults to `None` (as is [documented for numpy](https://docs.scipy.org/doc/numpy/reference/arrays.classes.html#numpy.class.__array_wrap__)), but the first line in `__array_wrap__` tries to unpack it unconditionally. I have no idea what the right behavior is in this case.

issue_title: dpkt.http.Response: server cookies not handled correctly
issue_body:
_From [simdr...@gmail.com](https://code.google.com/u/116904784437902388373/) on March 29, 2009 18:39:43_

What steps will reproduce the problem? data = """HTTP/1.x 200 OK\r\nSet-Cookie: first_cookie=cookie1; path=/;
domain=.example.com\r\nSet-Cookie: second_cookie=cookie2; path=/;
domain=.example.com\r\nContent-Length: 0\r\n\r\n"""
http = dpkt.http.Response(data)
print http What is the expected output? What do you see instead? Only the last server cookie "survives" [example script attached]

Original:
\----------------------------------------
HTTP/1.x 200 OK
Set-Cookie: first_cookie=cookie1; path=/; domain=.example.com
Set-Cookie: second_cookie=cookie2; path=/; domain=.example.com
Content-Length: 0
\----------------------------------------

Parsed:
\----------------------------------------
HTTP/1.x 200 OK
set-cookie: second_cookie=cookie2; path=/; domain=.example.com
content-length: 0
---------------------------------------- What version of the product are you using? On what operating system? dpkt 1.6 (Ubuntu + Windows XP), python 2.5.4 Please provide any additional information below. The problem is in dpkt.http.parse_headers() - it doesn't take into account
multiple headers with the same name and thus overwrites the value of
"set-cookie" with the value of the last "Set-Cookie" header.

Multiple cookies are very common in real world traffic (i.e. google.com,
facebook.com)
Fixing this bug would really improve this excellent module (thanks dug!)

**Attachment:** [dpkt_cookies.py](http://code.google.com/p/dpkt/issues/detail?id=18)

_Original issue: http://code.google.com/p/dpkt/issues/detail?id=18_

issue_title: Bind to OpenLDAP server with TLS
issue_body:
I have set up an OpenLDAP server which disallows anonymous binds and requires all connections to use TLS.  My LDAP server does not support SSL.  The server is listening on port 389 using the `ldap://` protocol, which is the recommended setup per this guide:

https://www.digitalocean.com/community/tutorials/how-to-encrypt-openldap-connections-using-starttls#ldap-over-ssl-vs-ldap-with-starttls

I have created a Python script which attempts to bind to my LDAP server with TLS.  The bind operation fails, and the server log indicates that the connection is not using TLS.

My Python script:
```
from ldap3 import Server, Connection, Tls
import ssl
import logging
from ldap3.utils.log import set_library_log_detail_level, EXTENDED

set_library_log_detail_level(EXTENDED)
logger = logging.getLogger('ldap3')
logger.setLevel(logging.DEBUG)
ch = logging.StreamHandler()
ch.setLevel(logging.DEBUG)
logger.addHandler(ch)

t = Tls(validate=ssl.CERT_REQUIRED, ca_certs_file="/etc/openldap/ca_certs.pem")
s = Server("ldap://ldap.mycompany.com:389", use_ssl=True, tls=t)
c = Connection(s, "uid=myuser,ou=Applications,dc=mycompany,dc=com", "mypassword")
c.bind()
```

issue_title: demo agent lists config for unsupported auth protocol / inconsistent naming
issue_body:
<http://snmplabs.com/snmpsim/public-snmp-agent-simulator.html> lists

`usr-sha384-aes256 | SHA2 (384/512bit) | authkey1 | AES (256bit) | privkey1`

But sha384 is not a supported. snmpget.py help reports:

`  -a AUTH-PROTOCOL  authentication protocol (MD5|NONE|SHA|SHA128|SHA192|SHA256|SHA512|SHA96)`

and trying to use SHA384:

```
snmpget.py -r 0 -v 3 -u usr-sha384-aes256 -x AES256 -X privkey1 -a SHA384 -A authkey1 -l authPriv 127.0.0.1:1161 sysContact.0
Error: Unknown authentication protocol "SHA384
```

I'm guessing that the config should be for **usr-sha512-aes256**.

Also, the previous line:

`usr-sha256-3des | SHA2 (128/224bit)`

doesn't make sense. 128/224 bits is usmHMAC128SHA224AuthProtocol so the name should be usr-sha224-3des.

It's all a bit confusing because in some cases you are using the key length and in others you are using the mac length.  It really should be "SHA224|SHA256|SHA384|SHA512" or "SHA128|SHA192|SHA256|SHA384". I'd suggest the former, as those are the SHA algorithms defined in RFC 6234 <https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6234> (and the key lengths). Mixing them is confusing, especially for SHA256, which could be the key length (256/192) or the mac length (384/256).

Both Net-SNMP and SNMP Research use the "SHA224|SHA256|SHA384|SHA512" forms.

issue_title: Improve documentation and install process for use in school environments
issue_body:
The problem with pip is it will by default try to install the package in a 'dist directory' which is mostly in a protected directory on school setups, so that means they can't use this lovely package.

If you documented other install procedures, more schools that have locked down IT systems will be able to use this.

I believe based on discussions just now on twitter that guizero is zero dependency. That means you ca do any of the following:

1. from github press the clone or download button, then download as zip. Unzip the zip file and copy the guizero folder into the students home folder, and then they should be able to just write python and import guizero and it *should* all work. This completely avoids bumping into any school protected directory issues.

2. If the school PC has write access to the distdir on the computer C drive, you might be able to run this from a command line:

python setup.py


My preference is (1) as in all the schools I work in, the dist-dir of python is in a read-only folder on the network or on the local disk and cannot be modified. 

The fact that the guizero folder is in the same folder as the children's code means that when you 'import guizero' it will find it (That's the purpose of the __init__.py in  guizero so it is recognised as a package folder).

Hope this helps - but it would be great if someone could update the README to describe this as the preferred method of installation, and offer the pip install option as an alternative route for schools that have much less locked-down environment.

hangups version: 0.4.2
aiohttp version: 2.2.0

Pullrequest #326 addresses this issue for raised Exceptions during handling a response, but with the latest aiohttp update (mainly https://github.com/aio-libs/aiohttp/issues/1985) the issue reached another level.

The cause for all the resource warnings: ``aiohttp==2.2.0`` does not close the ```aiohttp.ClientSession``` created in ```aiohttp.request()``` anymore.

issue_title: Adding a mechanism for progress update notification in long-lived executions
issue_body: I am working on a system where [celery](http://www.celeryproject.org) is used to queue and execute calibration tasks with spotpy. I would like to report to the user useful information as to the progress of the tasks. Currently the algorithms (SCE-UA) is printing statements to stdout. While reporting this as is, this is probably not suitable to reliably report progress (e.g. % of max runtime). I'll draft a design for an observer to plug in an object into SCE that can be updated with progress information, but if other users have suggestions as to designs, I'd like to hear. I'd prefer to reuse something already proven, though it may prove more complicated than a simpler, custom logging system.

issue_title: Add streaming support to handle large files
issue_body:
There is problem syncronizing large files , since its done all in memory , eventually you ll run out of it
Download to memory --> flush to disk

I implemented some kind of a streaming solution:

```python
class StreamingFileTarget(FsTarget):
   
    from functools import partial

    def write_file(self, name, source, blocksize=DEFAULT_BLOCKSIZE, callback=None):

        def write_stream_data(fp_dst, callback, data):
            fp_dst.write(data)
            if callback:
                callback(data)

        self.check_write(name)

        with open(os.path.join(self.cur_dir, name), "wb") as fp_dst:
            write_stream_partial = partial(write_stream_data, fp_dst, callback)
            source.read_stream(name, write_stream_partial)

class StreamingFtpTarget(FtpTarget):

    def read_stream(self, name, callback):
        self.ftp.retrbinary("RETR %s" % name, callback)

```
But ```def _copy_file``` needs to be alerted 

``` python
        dest.write_file(file_entry.name, src, callback=__block_written)
```

As noted in the Twisted changelog (https://github.com/twisted/twisted/blob/3df4e95b63f00eb92005301de1594218fc02e0cb/twisted/topfiles/NEWS#L62) and highlighted by ooni-probe continuous integration test (https://travis-ci.org/TheTorProject/ooni-probe/jobs/93784032) `IStreamClientEndpointStringParser` is deprecated in Twisted 15.5.0.
They recommend using `twisted.internet.interfaces.IStreamClientEndpointStringParserWithReactor`.

More details on the trac of twisted:

* http://twistedmatrix.com/trac/ticket/8094

* http://twistedmatrix.com/trac/ticket/5069

From the looks of it, it doesn't seem to be a complicated fix as the APIs seem mainly compatible one with the other, you just need to pass in the extra reactor argument.

Poll does not returns all the produced messages
issue_body:
Scenario: (all in a single process)
1. Create and start a consumer
2. Produce 4 messages
3. Poll for 4 messages (timeout can be low to very high)
Result poll always returns 2 messages.
4. 2nd poll returns remaining 2 messages.

Some observations:
1. Waiting after producing messages does not have any effect (i.e. messages are produced).
2. If we sleep for 1 second after starting the consumer (step 1) i.e. starting the consumer, the first poll returns 4 messages.
3. timeout in poll does not seem to an issue here.

issue_title: Consumer blocks indefinitely when calling poll()
issue_body:
We have a long running consumer process that consumes messages from a single partition kafka topic. There are 3 consumer instances in the group. About 1-2 times every day (these run for days at a time) all 3 consumer instances will all block within 2 minutes of one another. They block when they all call into poll(). A monitoring process restarts them after 10 minutes (after detecting they are blocking), and then the group will begin consuming again and catching up on old messages.

We call into poll with a timeout of 30000 ms. I understand that poll() will block on a rebalance but it seems like this is not the sole cause of a 10 minute block.

issue_title: Support extra time units in delay specification
issue_body:
At the moment when using the `--delay` option in `tcset`, you can only set time to milliseconds. According to the `tc` [man page ](http://lartc.org/manpages/tc.txt), it supports microseconds and seconds too. It would be great to have support for those time units plus the minutes which could be easily converted to one of the supported time units. 

Examples:

#### e.g. Set 100ms network latency
# tcset --device eth0 --delay 100ms

#### e.g. Set 100s network latency
# tcset --device eth0 --delay 100s

#### e.g. Set 1m network latency
# tcset --device eth0 --delay 1m

Thanks,
Pavlos

Several modules have threshold and I think we should look at trying to standardize how these are treated. 

__names__

Sometime they are things like `critical`, `high_threshold`, `threshold_high`.  I think we should look at unifying them as  `threshold_xxx`

Alternatively we could fully switch to the `thresholds = {..}` approach see later

Sometime they are things like `threshold_high` other times `threshold_bad` I prefer the more descriptive names  `threshold_high`

__showing thresholds__

Currently there is no system for this that I can see. Options could be

* set `threshold_xxx = None` to disable (this would mean changing all modules)

*  Use `colors` parameter that is applied in core that strips any added colors from a modules output.  This could be added to any module or on the general section. it would work as it does for i3status. This would be my preference.

__custom thresholds__

vnstat allows fully customized thresholds which is an interesting idea as it give the user full flexibility
`coloring = { 800: "#dddd00", 900: "#dd0000", }` 

I think this is an interesting idea that we should look into more (maybe allowing named colors) eg
`thresholds = { 800: "good", 900: "degraded", 1000: '#FF00FF' }` 

__multiple thresholds__

sysdata has thresholds that it uses for more than one use cpu/memory and realistically is missing ones for cpu temp. options are

* multiple params - `threshold_cpu_high`

* using a dict `thresholds = {'cpu': {'high': 95, 'low': 50 }, 'memory': {...}}`

* split dicts `thresholds_cpu = {'high': 95, 'low': 50 }` or even `thresholds_cpu = {'high': (95, 'bad'), 'low': (50, '#FFFF00') }`

Currently there is a problem about which value to care about PR #449, #439 try to solve this by adding `threshold_value` parameter to say which is to be used.  To me this feels the wrong solution as we should just use the components functionality to allow cpu/memory/cpu temp to be colored independently. 

__helpers__

It might well be that adding some helpers into py3 would help keep consistency between modules and potentially allow us to deal with the above issues in more than one way eg

```
module {
    thresholds_cpu = {'high': (95, 'bad'), 'low': (50, 'good') }
}
```
would be the same as
```
module {
    thresholds_cpu_high = 95
    thresholds_cpu_low = 50
    color_thresholds_cpu_high = 'bad'
    color_thresholds_cpu_low = 'good'
}
```

Anyway the main thing is getting a consistent approach that we can use across all relevant modules.  We can start small and increase the features/flexibility as we go.

issue_title: TypeError while logging to file with --debug
issue_body:
Just a minor issue while logging debug info to a file I noticed this:

> 2016-10-05 19:07:50 WARNING Instance `netdata`, user method `net_speed` failed (TypeError) core.py line 497.

```
TypeError: write() argument must be str, not bytes
  File "/usr/lib/python3.5/site-packages/py3status/module.py", line 496, in run
    'method {} returned {} '.format(meth, result))
  File "/usr/lib/python3.5/site-packages/py3status/core.py", line 497, in log
    f.write(out.encode('utf-8'))
```

The unicode arrows used by the netdata module are causing the issue. I found adding `encoding='utf8'` to the open() in core.py log method fixes this issue for python3 if you don't mind the unprintable chars in your file.  Python2 needs io.open() to support the encoding parameter though. Since both versions can use io.open() I think it might the best way to avoid TypeError and UnicodeEncodeError for modules like this.

As a side note I'm not sure how good or bad writing utf8 to a file is since open() uses the filesystem encoding setting as the default.  Its different than the default encoding on my system at least.
```
>>> sys.getdefaultencoding()
'utf-8'
>>> sys.getfilesystemencoding()
'ascii'
```
So instead of encoding='utf8' in io.open() if you just use errors='backslashreplace' you will have nice ascii printable unicode escape codes in your file.

If you think the solution is good I can create another pull request.

This value should be documented in the README file, as first time users who want to view log files created more than 5 minutes ago will not see any logs. An alternative would be to print out all of the parameters supplied at the start of the `get` printout.

Also, to me this value also seems very low for a default - 1 or 2 days would be more appropriate.

Dual stack server, working on regular internet (TCP and SSL) and on Tor it should work only on TCP (50001) because a) Tor hidden services are already self authenticated and end-to-end encrypted and b) the SSL certificate does not contain the onion hostname so it would never actually work anyway.

The server is connected to IRC with _tor suffix, but still reports a SSL port to 50002 on the onion hostname regardless REPORT_SSL_PORT_TOR = 0 is set in electrumx.conf

Hey Nick,

Few things.

1. When I run the webpack-dev-server with `webpack-dev-server --config webpack.config.js --content-base build/public`, it tells me no `manifest.json` file found. I am using the ManifestRevisionPlugin as follows:

```javascript
plugins: [
    new ManifestRevisionPlugin(path.join(__dirname, 'build', 'manifest.json'), {
      rootAssetPath: rootAssetPath,
      ignorePaths: ['/js']
    })
  ]
```

In solution, I run `webpack --build --config webpack.config.js --progress`, which builds my assets, and creates the `manifest.json` file. When I then run the dev server, everything works as it should.

2. I'm confused as to how to production-ize my assets. Of course, I was able to build them as per Step 1. My web server itself is Flask, and I have a bunch of React and html code. With `webpack --build --config webpack.config.js --progress`, I'm only building 2 `.js` files, which I referencing in other places as, for example, `{{ javascript_tag('info_js') | safe }}`.

When I'm not running the dev server, I cannot figure out how to point Flask to the built versions of these 2 `.js` files. My app lives within app_name/app_name, and my `build` folder exists in the root directory of the project (`app_name/`). Of course, the `publicPath` is no longer `localhost:webpack_dev_server_port`. 

To what do I change the `publicPath`? How do I get my Flask server to look in this new `publicPath`? I ultimately want to deploy this on Heroku. My intuition was to ship the raw project, have Heroku build it, and then reference the 2 built-with-webpack `.js` files in `app_name/build/public/`.

Any and all help is appreciated. Thanks!

issue_title: Installation fix in #19 was not uploaded to pypi

```bash
pip3 install git+https://github.com/invernizzi/scapy-http@df0caa6f38a88e45f64dff0bb3cdfaceee270ae2 -r ./requirements.txt
```
The above command meanwhile has the intended results.

According to PyPI the most recent version is [4.1.16](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/stomp.py/4.1.16) but the most recent tag in this repo is v4.1.13.

Please tag later releases (or push tags).

issue_title: Can we generate the client code for my swagger definition file just like swagger-codegen
issue_body:
Now I think I can use this project to test my definition file directly by:
load json
send request
access response.

is it correct?

But I want to generate a client SDK for other's call my api to do the test.
I know swagger-codegen can generate a client code and can be used with calling api_client.
how can I realize it with this project.

Currently the default valueInputOption value is 'USER_ENTERED' on the sh_update_range and sh_append.

I would like to be able to override the default value of valueInputOption, so that all sheet operations update with 'RAW' by default.

This would allow for functions like Worksheet.update_row to operate in 'RAW' and still omit the 'parse' argument on the update_cells call.

issue_title: SymbolicTransformer does not create added value features as expected
issue_body:
Hi @trevorstephens ,

I am not sure if this is a bug, or the documentation is not correct focused refered to SymbolicTransformer.
I have done a show case of how SymbolicRegressor works and predicts well the equation that represents the dataset, while SymbolicTransformer does not work in the same way.

Starting with SymbolicRegressor, I have done a "easy" dataset to check if SymbolicRegressor give me the correct result and good metrics.

```
from gplearn.genetic import SymbolicRegressor
from sklearn import metrics
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

# Load data
X = np.random.uniform(0,100,size=(100,3))
y = np.min(X[:,:2],axis=1)*X[:,2]

index = 80
X_train , y_train = X[:index,:], y[:index]
X_test , y_test = X[index:,:], y[index:]

function_set = ['add', 'sub', 'mul', 'div', 'sqrt', 'log',
                'abs', 'neg', 'inv', 'max', 'min', 'sin', 'cos', 'tan']

est_gp = SymbolicRegressor(population_size=5000,
                           generations=20, stopping_criteria=0.001,
                           function_set=function_set,
                           p_crossover=0.7, p_subtree_mutation=0.1,
                           p_hoist_mutation=0.05, p_point_mutation=0.1,
                           max_samples=0.9, verbose=1,
                           n_jobs=1,
                           parsimony_coefficient=0.01, random_state=0)
est_gp.fit(X_train, y_train)

print 'Score: ', est_gp.score(X_test, y_test), metrics.mean_absolute_error(y_test, est_gp.predict(X_test))
print est_gp._program
```

This example give us a perfect result and the MAE metrics is ~perfect as shows the output:

```
    |    Population Average   |             Best Individual              |
---- ------------------------- ------------------------------------------ ----------
 Gen   Length          Fitness   Length          Fitness      OOB Fitness  Time Left
   0    11.81    8396.89543051       10    25.3022470326     26.608049431     35.35s
   1    12.36    8904.35549713        8    20.0284767508    19.0994923956     37.34s
   2    13.74     37263.312834        8 7.82583874247e-14 2.13162820728e-14     36.67s
Score:  1.0 5.71986902287e-14
abs(div(neg(X2), inv(min(X0, X1))))
```

However, SymbolicTransformer although the training works well, the transform does not work well.
See next same example to previous one but with SymbolicTransformer:

```
from gplearn.genetic import SymbolicRegressor,SymbolicTransformer
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from sklearn import linear_model
from sklearn import metrics

X = np.random.uniform(0,100,size=(100,3))
y = np.min(X[:,:2],axis=1)*X[:,2]

index = 80
X_train , y_train = X[:index,:], y[:index]
X_test , y_test = X[index:,:], y[index:]

# Linear model - Original features
est_lin = linear_model.Lars()
est_lin.fit(X_train, y_train)
print 'Lars(orig): ', est_lin.score(X_test, y_test), metrics.mean_absolute_error(y_test, est_lin.predict(X_test))

# Create added value features
function_set = ['add', 'sub', 'mul', 'div', 'sqrt', 'log',
                'abs', 'neg', 'inv', 'max', 'min']

gp = SymbolicTransformer(generations=20, population_size=2000,
                         hall_of_fame=100, n_components=10,
                         function_set=function_set,
                         parsimony_coefficient=0.0005,
                         max_samples=0.9, verbose=1,
                         random_state=0, n_jobs=3)

gp.fit(X_train, y_train)
gp_features = gp.transform(X)

# Linear model - Transformed features
newX = np.hstack((X, gp_features))
print 'newX: ', np.shape(newX)
est_lin = linear_model.Lars()
est_lin.fit(newX[:index,:], y_train)
print 'Lars(trans): ', est_lin.score(newX[index:,:], y_test), metrics.mean_absolute_error(y_test, est_lin.predict(newX[index:,:]))

# Linear model - "The" feature
newX = np.append(X, (np.min(X[:,:2],axis=1)*X[:,2]).reshape(-1,1), axis=1)
print 'newX: ', newX.shape
est_lin = linear_model.Lars()
est_lin.fit(newX[:index,:], y_train)
print 'Lars(trans): ', est_lin.score(newX[index:,:], y_test), metrics.mean_absolute_error(y_test, est_lin.predict(newX[index:,:]))
```

I use Lars from sklearn for avoid Ridge sparse weights, and find the best solution fast for this easy and exact example. As it can be seen on the results of this code (below), the features that are generated with transform, although during the fit fitness become perfect, the added transformed features seem to be worng. The problem does not come from Lars, as last example of Lars shows that adding "the feature" which is the target, the accuracy is perfetc.

```
X:  (100, 3)
y:  (100,)
Lars(orig):  0.850145084161 518.34496409

I'm going to open a new issue so it doesn't get lost. This was originally posted in issue https://github.com/andreafrancia/trash-cli/issues/13#issuecomment-45187738

I understand that trash-cli cannot trash files when the trash and the file are on seperate partitions. However, I'm now running into the opposite of this. I have my trash on HDD, and my home on SDD. This time, I can only trash files that are on the SDD, and not those on the HDD (the same partition as the trash). $ cd ~
$ touch foo
$ trash foo # works for files on a different partition.
$ cd ~/HDD
$ touch foo
$ trash foo # fails for files on the same partition.
trash: cannot trash regular empty file `foo'
$ trash ./foo # fails for this format
trash: cannot trash regular empty file `./foo'
$ trash ~/HDD/foo # works if I specify relative to home directory, with "semi-absolute" path.
$ touch foo
$ cd ..
$ trash HDD/foo # works again for this relative path.
$ cd ~/HDD
$ touch foo
$ readlink -e foo
/HDD/protist/foo
$ trash /HDD/protist/foo # fails again for totally absolute path without symlinks.
trash: cannot trash regular empty file `/HDD/protist/foo'
```

I've worked around this by defining a trash wrapper in my `$PATH`, ahead of `/usr/bin/trash`. This works well, and now allows trashing of all files, no matter what partition they are on! I've put this in `/usr/local/bin/trash`

```
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Workaround for trash-cli not being able to trash files on the *same* partition.

for i in "$@"; do
  fullpath="$(readlink -e "$i")"
  fixedpath="$(echo "$fullpath" | sed "s,/HDD/$USER,$HOME/HDD,")"
  /usr/bin/trash "$fixedpath"
done
```

issue_title: Cannot install django-choices due to __version__ import that was added setup.py
issue_body:
When running `pip install django-choices` in a clean virtualenv I'm getting this error:
```
  Downloading django-choices-1.4.tar.gz
    Complete output from command python setup.py egg_info:
    Traceback (most recent call last):
      File "<string>", line 20, in <module>
      File "/tmp/pip-build-EUbJR5/django-choices/setup.py", line 11, in <module>
        from djchoices import __version__
      File "djchoices/__init__.py", line 1, in <module>
        from djchoices.choices import ChoiceItem, DjangoChoices, C
      File "djchoices/choices.py", line 2, in <module>
        from django.core.exceptions import ValidationError
    ImportError: No module named django.core.exceptions
    
```
The traceback shows that setup.py imports `__version__`, and this causes a premature import of djchoices.choices .

The bad import was added here: https://github.com/bigjason/django-choices/commit/9e640f6dd89a9824e0fd8e524a53069f57b7f017

I think that if you want to import the version in setup.py, you should move it to a separate file (one that does not cause djchoices.choices to get imported)

issue_title: Use Redis Cache Expiration to Minimize Memory Usage (or Encourage a Cron job)
issue_body:
Unless I'm missing something, enabling this app will slowly but steadily take up more and more memory/hd space as more and more IP addresses visit your site. I can see two solutions for this:

1. For those that use Redis for their cache, we can use [the EXPIRE command][1] to make it so keys go away when they're no longer relevant. For example, if the rate is 5/hour and the last time an IP hit was an hour ago, we can expire the key.

1. For those that don't use Redis, we should encourage a cron job, similar to [the one that's used to clear old old sessions][sess].

As it is now, we have neither of these things and this app is essentially a memory leak for those using an in-memory cache. Not great.

[1]: https://redis.io/commands/expire
[sess]: https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/1.11/topics/http/sessions/#clearing-the-session-store

Mainly a question: for multipart uploads, should we try to have `tell()` return the position in the remote file instead of the local buffer? If so, I could try to work on this. With the current behavior, writing `zipfile.ZipFile`s larger than `AWS_S3_FILE_BUFFER_SIZE` to an underlying `S3Boto3StorageFile` breaks because the built-in Python ZIP library [depends upon](https://github.com/python/cpython/blob/5223ce2131a121201a78d0680302ea06c4a58369/Lib/zipfile.py#L1331) `tell()` returning the position with respect to the entire file.

Example of `tell()` resetting after flushing the buffer:

issue_title: Mismatch in proportional hazards between R and Mathematica with Lifelines
issue_body:
I'm working with a dataset with one covariate that is categorical and has three levels. In R and Mathematica I get a proportional hazard of 1.5 (coefficient of 0.41649) but in Lifelines I get a hazard of 0.8899 (coefficient of -0.1166). My workflow is as follows:

- Import original data set
-Create an event array (1's for failure and 0's for right censorship)
-Create a duration array
-Use pandas pd.factorize to create a number dummy array to represent the three covariates
-Combine the event, duration and dummy array into a new pandas dataframe
---At this point the dataframe has:
----event array 479 non-null float64
----duration array 479 non-null float64
----dummy array 479 non-null int
-Calculate the proportional hazard using the new dataframe:
cph.fit(df, duration_col='time', event_col='event', show_progress=True)

The output shows that convergence is completed after 5 iterations, and shows the correct number of events and n but is reporting a different coef than R. If I take the same dataframe and export it to csv and then run it in R then I get a proportional hazard of 1.5. This is also the case with Mathematica.

Anyone have any suggestions for troubleshooting this? Thanks!

issue_title: Checkpoint restores second to last generation
issue_body:
Hello,

If for example I set the checkpointer to save every 5 generations, the checkpoint is made at the end of generation 4, and then generation 5 starts.

If I restore checkpoint-4, generation 4 starts again, and a checkpoint-4 is made again.

Is this by design?

Because I am now running a fairly big population which takes about an hour for the generation to complete. The checkpoints are every generation. But every time I interrupt and restore, I am losing a generation's worth of progress.

I have made a dirty fix for this: to make a checkpoint at the start of the generation instead of the end. It's not ideal, because after moving the contents of "end_generation" function to "start_generation" I had to add all the parameters to the "start_generation" function in reporting.py and population.py.

But otherwise it works:

" ****** Running generation 4 ****** 

Saving checkpoint to neat-checkpoint-4"

I am sure you will be able to implement a more elegant solution if, of course, this fix is warranted.

Thanks!

I've been thinking this over for a bit now...  I found that what I at first considered a bug is probably better described as a design decision.  I'm looking for input from people to see what they think...

Basically, the colander schema you use differs if you're generating a form for **adding** a new object then if you were **editing** an object.  For example, if you're adding a new object with an incremental primary key, you probably want to have `missing=colander.drop` so that the backend SQL system fills in the value with the next incremental value.  However, if you are modifying/editing that same object it doesn't make as much sense.  In the editing case a person blanking out that field would mean it would make no change to the field, but what you probably want is `missing=colander.required` as you'd want the validator to trip when the person tried to null a primary key field.

Right now ColanderAlchemy has a focus on the right settings for **adding** only.  I was thinking it might be helpful to add an argument that you pass to specify which defaults to use (whether "add" or "edit"), but that's an enhancement for another time.  For right now, I think it's best to continue the focus on "add only" but make it so the schema has as little dissimilarity with the "edit" schema as possible.  To that end, I'd like to make nullable fields set to `missing=colander.null`.

Here's a test case that currently fails that I'd like to make work:
```python
    def test_null_issues(self):
        """ Make sure nullable values are set properly when null
        
        #42 introduced an issue where values may not have been properly
        set if the value is supposed to be set to null
        """
        Base = declarative_base()

        class Person(Base):
            __tablename__ = 'person'
            id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
            fullname = Column(String, nullable=True)
            age = Column(Integer, nullable=True)

        schema = SQLAlchemySchemaNode(Person)

        person = Person(
            id=7,
            fullname="Joe Smith",
            age=35,
        )

        # dict coming from a form submission
        update_cstruct = {
            'id': '7',
            'fullname': '',
            'age': '',
        }
        update_appstruct = schema.deserialize(update_cstruct)
        schema.objectify(update_appstruct, context=person)

        self.assertEqual(person.id, 7)
        self.assertEqual(person.fullname, None)
        self.assertEqual(person.age, None)
```

issue_title: Improve Oauth2 API for handling tokens persistence with SQL
issue_body:
I'm trying to accommodate storing my tokens in an SQL database, and trying to extend the `OAuth2` class to enable storing and retrieving the tokens from the database. The `_refresh_lock` is something that is naturally also managed by the same database. (You have a conceptually similar implementation for redis with `RedisManagedOAuth2`)

What you would typically do with SQL in order to lock a specific row to update it (exactly what we want to do here) is use the `SELECT ... FOR UPDATE` command, which locks and retrieves the data at the same time

The existing code where the lock is used (https://github.com/box/box-python-sdk/blob/6597e930929f3ee49d4930a53304277167c0a4db/boxsdk/auth/oauth2.py#L201-L202 and https://github.com/box/box-python-sdk/blob/6597e930929f3ee49d4930a53304277167c0a4db/boxsdk/auth/oauth2.py#L305-L306) looks like this :

```python
def refresh():
  with self.refresh_lock:
    tokens = self.get_tokens()
    # ... proceed with logic to update them
```

What I'm looking for is this

```python
def refresh():
  with self.select_for_update(...) as tokens:
    # ... proceed with logic
```

Well, I can't really extend the class to achieve that unless I rewrite the whole methods `refresh` and `revoke`. I think a better API to express the need to "lock tokens and retrieve them" is with its own context manager function, that can have a default implementation that can be overridden. This contextmanager can be trivially rewritten to use select for update

```python
def refresh():
  with self.lock_tokens(...) as tokens:
    # ... proceed with logic

@contextmanager
def lock_tokens():
    with self.refresh_lock:
      yield self.get_tokens()
```

When `merge_multi_context=True` in a call to `get_outputs` of a `DataParallelExecutorGroup`, `_merge_multi_context` will try to concatenate the outputs from the different devices along the major axis.

The major axis is computed based on `[DataDesc.get_batch_axis(self.symbol[name].attr('__layout__')) for name in self.output_names]` in the initializer of `DataParallelExecutorGroup`.

What is the recommended way to set the `attr('__layout__')` of a symbol? Simply pass `attr={'__layout__': layout}` when constructing the symbol? Can the attribute be set automatically during module binding?

Setting `attr('__layout__')` is necessary, as it is otherwise `None`, leading to `_merge_multi_context` trying to concatenate along `dim=0` which will fail if the batch size is not divisible by the number of devices and the symbol outputs a shape (1, batch_size_per_device, X).

I.e. in case of 3 devices and batch size 128, concatenating `(1, 43), (1, 43), (1, 42)` along `dim=0` will fail.

1) It seems that for some reason psycopg2 unable to autoconvert Error to string. It can be fixed in jobs.py/import_errors function
```python
    def import_errors(self, dagbag):
        session = settings.Session()
        session.query(models.ImportError).delete()
        for filename, stacktrace in list(dagbag.import_errors.items()):
            session.add(models.ImportError(
                filename=filename, stacktrace=str(stacktrace)))
        session.commit()
```
but i think it's not the best solution.

2) Also, scheduler is falling despite it should never ever stop. Nonetheless it can be fixed by wrapping body of while loop in additional try except block
```python
while not self.num_runs or self.num_runs > i:
    try:
        <while body>
    except Exception e:
        pass
```
but once again, may be there is better solution.
http://bravado-core.readthedocs.org/en/latest/models.html#model-discovery is stale and needs to be updated.

It would be nice if it would be possible to import with aliases with the `as` keyword, like

```python
SHELL_PLUS_POST_IMPORTS = (
    (('datetime', 'dt'),),
)
```
it would mean the same as:
`import datetime as dt`

it would be backward compatible if the second element of param tuples would be the alias(es).
another example:

```python
SHELL_PLUS_PRE_IMPORTS = (
    ('module.submodule1', (('class1', 'class1_alias'), ('function2', 'function2_alias'))),
    ('module.submodule2', (('function3', 'function3_alias'),)),
    ('module.submodule3', '*'),
    (('module.submodule4', 'submodule4_alias'),),
)
```
would be equivalent to:
```python
from module.submodule1 import class1 as class1_alias, function2 as function2_alias
from module.submodule2 import function3 as function3_alias
from module.submodule3 import *
import module.submodule4 as submodule4_alias
```

Or maybe some other syntax would be better because my eyes hurt writing all these parenthese :smile:

As of jQuery 3.0, `jQuery("#")` throws an error since it's an invalid selector, rather than the previous behavior of silently returning an empty set. Need a warning about this and to have it return the aforementioned empty set.

As of jQuery 3.0, disconnected or non-element input to `.offset` will no longer return `{ top: 0, left: 0 }`, and may throw. Migrate should restore functionality changed by https://github.com/jquery/jquery/commit/1617479fcf7cbdaf33dc9334ed10a0f30bf14687 / https://github.com/jquery/jquery/commit/d4dd548acaf049d8e4dca9e4b879a51f26bb3d27, and warn when there is no layout box.

The `@items` service filters out the result based on permissions.
The pagination is made prior to filtering, leading to exotic paging information in the final result.

issue_title: Importers can't keep up with the GPU, and slow down the training
issue_body:
On IRC we agreed that fully preprocessing the datasets, including calculating the mfcc features, is probably the way to go:

1. As a first step, change our importers to write all the labels into a single file instead of one txt file for each wav. This should reduce our time spent opening and closing tons of small files. This single file can be a CSV or JSON where each entry is of the format ```"audio": "wav file name", "transcript": "the quick brown fox…"```, for example.
2. Add an additional step to the importers that computes and saves the MFCCs so that we don't have to do that during feeding.
3. Refactor the audio related code to do format conversion, channel splitting, segment splitting and feature calculation in a single pass to avoid reopening of small files. This is probably going to result in cleaner code as well: we won't have to use the filesystem as state for the importer steps.
4. Move the Unicode normalization code from the DataSet into the data munging part of the importer. It was added to the DataSet as a hack to avoid having to delete the processed corpus files and run the importer again.

This should get us to a state where we do almost no work on the input data during feeding of the network, and remove the importers as a bottleneck for training. Any comments on this plan?
They're no longer compatible with the current graph and we're unlikely to ever use them. If we decide to revive them in the future they'll always be in source control.

issue_title: conf.d-style loading of a bag of files
issue_body:
One of the things discussed with the [downstream](https://github.com/jupyter/notebook/pull/879) is a [conf.d](https://lists.debian.org/debian-devel/2010/04/msg00352.html)-style loading of a directory of files, probably put there by a package manager (at least, not put there by an application itself).

It could live here. This would have the benefit of making all ConfigManager downstream implementations able to use this feature. There would also be the question of whether one could silently add this.

If it lived here, it looks like it would have to be reflected in:
- a FileConfigLoader subclass
- a BaseJSONConfigManager

> **Question** Why do both of these even exist?

For example, in the notebook, this would entail loading and merging the following files:
- `[sys.prefix | /usr/local | ~)/jupyter/
  - `jupyter_notebook_config.json.d/`
    0. some_thing.json
    1. some_other_thing.json
  - `jupyter_notebook_config.json`

When writing in both those files, when a config is _written_, it can only write to the "non-.d" location. Further, it shouldn't write out the whole shooting match (of all the `.d/*` files), but only the novel, unique data introduced by the user. so:

```
conda install nbpresent
jupyter nbextension disble --py some_other_extension
```

shouldn't store the nbpresent data in the config. This suggests we may need some kind of "taint checking" to know whether a value should be persisted or not.

issue_title: "Too Many Files Open" Error - Files Not Being Closed?
issue_body:
I've been using the ANNOY Python API to build and save several index tables in order to load and reuse subsets of these in another script (at most about 400 in a subset at a time). The library is wonderfully fast, but I've been encountering "too many files open" errors after some time. I'm loading each index once, doing stuff with it, then unloading it before the loop continues onwards with the next index table.

Here is a partial code snippet that reproduces the error by reloading the same index table (510 x 64) over and over (run the loop multiple times if the error doesn't show up immediately):

	from annoy import AnnoyIndex
	index_table = AnnoyIndex( 64,metric='angular' )
	for i in xrange(10000):
		index_table.load( index_table_filename )
		print i  # just seeing when it failed
		index_table.unload( index_table_filename )

After some sleuthing into the GitHub source code, I notice that unload() unmaps the file from memory, but doesn't close the file opened using load(). Would it be possible to get file closing functionality incorporated into the Python wrapper somehow please? Or am I missing something vital in using ANNOY?

Thanks for all the hard work on the API!

issue_title: Tweet string doesn't appear for draft photo posts
issue_body:
I've had a program that works properly for a good year or two now that recently (since August 17th) experienced a problem.

The program does some things to generate a few images, then creates a draft photo post with those images, some description text, and a tweet with some summary text. Up until August 16th, the tweet text came through fine and I could queue the draft post via tumblr's web UI - just to be clear this was the intended behavior.

After that date, the tweet text does not appear to show up in the draft properly on the web UI - when I view it, it's the same auto-generated-from-description-text tweet that any post gets, and the twitter post button isn't enabled by default whereas it was before.

Here's the part of my program's code that compiles the tweet string and submits the draft post:

issue_title: How to deploy ranged version for library
issue_body:
Hello.

I differential python library to "application". A library ends up typically on pypi to be used by another application. One should never do a `pip install thislibrary` in production. Should comes from a `pip install thisapplication` that consequently install other apps and libraries.

For me, application should indeed have frozen dependencies, to ensure perfect reproduction. But libraries deployed on pypi should better be using "range" for acceptable version. For exemple, if two libraries depends on frozen version of another lib (say `1.0` and `2.0`), there will be a conflict. Libraries dependencies are better described with range (I know my lib will work with this dependencies from version `1.0` to `3.0`, or require a version `>1.1`, and so on).

Is it possible to do this with the pipfile and how? It is possible to describe this subtility in the documentation? "How to handle dependencies of a library or an application" ?

Thanks

issue_title: easy_install: use --depth 1 with git clone when downloading requirements
issue_body:
Currently, when fetching requirements via `git`, `easy_install` fetches the complete git history. This can take very long for large repos.

To fix this, we can use `--depth 1` when doing `git clone` and subsequently do a `git fetch --depth 1 origin <rev>` to download a specific revision.

I already prepared a [branch](https://github.com/pypa/setuptools/compare/master...ubunatic:shallow-git-clone?expand=1) to fix this, and I am preparing a PR just now (following the PR instructions).

I tested my branch already with a private repo using
- `python3.6 -m easy_install --user -v git+ssh://git@github.com/private/repo.git@master`
- `python3.6 -m easy_install --user -v git+ssh://git@github.com/private/repo.git`

to check the `rev is None` and `rev is not None` code-paths.

issue_title: Please add support for default UNIX "date" command format
issue_body:
Most Unix/Linux flavors use **`%a %b %e %T %Z %Y`** as the default date format.  However dateparser does not support that format.

    > date
    Tue Oct 13 20:18:56 CDT 2015
    
    > python
    >>> import dateparser
    >>> type(dateparser.parse('Tue Oct 13 20:18:56 CDT 2015'))
    <class 'NoneType'>

[Tornado](https://technobeans.com/2012/09/17/tornado-file-uploads/), [Flask](http://flask.pocoo.org/docs/0.12/patterns/fileuploads/), [Django](https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/1.11/topics/http/file-uploads/), and most current web frameworks allows for convenient functions for uploading files to the backend. Klein should handle file uploads in a similar fashion as well. Here's a quick example of how it could work:

``` python
@route('/')
def example(request):
    # example Request.files
    file_0 = request.files['my_files'][0]
    file_1 = request.files['my_files'][1]

    # example of what a "HTTP File" object would contain
    file_0.filename  # str
    file_0.content    # BytesIO
```

This issue cannot be addressed without bringing up the infamous [Issue 288](http://twistedmatrix.com/trac/ticket/288). There are some great ideas in the comments of that issue. One comment suggests processing args after the request object has been created. Building upon that, we should be able to implement a solution for files (at one point when `tx.web2` was a thing, it seems that this was a possible). This would involve extending `web.Request` or creating a Klein specific Request component. I'm just throwing this idea up here in case anyone has suggestions or has anything.

As a contingency plan, another project could be created and it could replicate, for instance, what [Tornado does for files](https://github.com/tornadoweb/tornado/blob/master/tornado/httputil.py#L749).

issue_title: Ensure rdkafka logging feeds into pykafka loggers

issue_body: [This comment](https://github.com/Parsely/pykafka/pull/480#issuecomment-199502760) indicates that at very high message sizes, snappy compression will fail under PyPy. Large message size compression should be tested in `pykafka/tests/pykafka/utils/test_compression.py` for all interpreters.

issue_title: balancedconsumer fails to reown partitions in case of network failures
issue_body:
Hello, 

We experience from time to time problems with balanced consumer: in case of network failures, some partitions left unconsumed. 
Here is an example, we are running two consumers in a group for a topic with 6 partitions. Normally each takes care of 3 partitions. In this gist: 
https://gist.github.com/tigran-a/c93ca879df25624120d09b15e5019952
you can see, that after connection issues, the consumer "lost" one partition. 
The other consumer "lost" 2 partitions.

If I stop both consumers and start them again, all partitions are used. 

Is there any reasonable workaround for that? 
E.g. periodically try to refresh/reown unowned partitions or so? 

**PyKafka version**:  2.4.0
**Kafka version**: 0.8.2.2

Thanks for any help!

issue_title: Allow for custom retry amount, delay, and timeout.
issue_body: Lately our calls to GoCardless are taking an excessive amount of time and are even causing timeouts in our wsgi. Calls to [`BaseService. _perform_request`](https://github.com/gocardless/gocardless-pro-python/blob/c97b66fa88685b06d1d8e13908a78e3cf0cde9f1/gocardless_pro/services/base_service.py#L31-L41) are all using hardcoded settings, e.g: [`RedirectFlowsService.create`](https://github.com/gocardless/gocardless-pro-python/blob/c97b66fa88685b06d1d8e13908a78e3cf0cde9f1/gocardless_pro/services/redirect_flows_service.py#L37-L39). It would be appreciated if we could configure these ourselves someone and also configure a timeout. The library uses requests internally which allows you to pass a [timeout](http://docs.python-requests.org/en/master/user/quickstart/#timeouts), it would be neat if we could pass all this data ourselves and handle the timeout and other exceptions ourselves.

issue_title: Update datasets module to use data.astropy.org
issue_body:
I copied over most of the data files from photutils-datasets to data.astropy.org:

https://github.com/astropy/astropy-data/pull/17

So at some point we can update the references in the datasets module.

I tried to create the documentation for a project with python 3.5 and received some warnings.

Sphinx wasn't able to resolve `collections.Iterable`, `collections.Generator`, `collections.Iterator`, `collections.Callable` and `collections.MutableMapping`. This is almost the exact problem I had in #237 but with other types.

issue_title: skimage FTBFS on multiple architectures testsuite failures.
issue_body:
## Description
When building skimage on Debian autobuilders the test suite fails on several architectures.
In the [bug log](https://bugs.debian.org/cgi-bin/bugreport.cgi?bug=840589#35) it is explained that when checking with gdb it is just by chance that the failure does not happen with amd64.  The problematic commit is d29831882daef9b7d7691becc3669349d642b547.  The variable firstbg is only initialized conditionally in the first loop. It depends on the array values and the and background value. Still it is used as the starting pointer on the second loop, even if uninitialized. At best the pointer point to the wrong address, at worst it generates an out of bound access causing the segmentation fault.

## Way to reproduce
See [bug log](https://bugs.debian.org/cgi-bin/bugreport.cgi?bug=840589#35)

- [ ] Debian Linux 
- [ ] 0.12.3

issue_title: hough_circle_peaks absent from conda and pip installations?
issue_body:
## Description
Can't import hough_circle_peaks from skimage.transform

## Way to reproduce
Install scikit-image from anaconda and:
```
>>> from skimage.transform import hough_circle_peaks
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
ImportError: cannot import name 'hough_circle_peaks'
```
I've tried in a different anaconda environment to install it with pip but got the same import error.
Upon closer inspection of `transform.py` in `anaconda3/pkgs/scikit-image-0.12.3-np111py35_1/lib/python3.5/site-packages/skimage/transform` I've found no hough_circle_peaks function.
Is it possible to directly replace the (outdated?) `transform.py` by the one in this scikit-image github (didn't seem to work)?
If not, how to install scikit-image directly from source in a given anaconda environment?
- [ ] Operating system and version
linux, ubuntu 16.04
- [ ] Python version
3.5
- [ ] scikit-image version (run `skimage.__version__`)
```
>>> import skimage
>>> skimage.__version__
'0.12.3'
```

issue_title: Use external Qt compatibility layer for viewer
issue_body:
We currently use the Matplotlib Qt interface to achieve compatibility with PySide + Qt4 + Qt5, but their compatibility backend is not intended for external consumption.

Instead, consider moving to a library such as:

https://github.com/mottosso/Qt.py

See also this StackOverflow question: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/47640724/pyqt5-has-issue-while-importing-in-skimage

# 运行环境: 
> - python: `3.6`
> - windows: `10` & ubuntu: `16`
> - aliyun-python-sdk-core-v3: `2.8.6` 
> - aliyun-python-sdk-ros: `2.2.7`

# 错误重现:
以DescribeStacksRequest类举例,DeleteStackRequest也是相同问题.
``` python
from aliyunsdkros.request.v20150901 import DescribeStacksRequest
    def get_stacks(self,regionId='cn-shenzhen',stackId=None,status=None,name=None):
        '''
        查询资源栈列表
        :param stackId: 资源栈 ID
        :param status: 用于过滤的资源栈状态。
        :param name: 资源栈名称
        :return:
        '''
        client = aliyunsdkcore.client.AcsClient(ak=self.ak,secret=self.secret,region_id=regionId)
        req = DescribeStacksRequest.DescribeStacksRequest()
        if stackId:
            req.set_StackId(StackId=stackId)
        if status:
            req.set_Status(Status=status)
        if name:
            req.set_Name(Name=name)
        req.set_PageSize(self.pagesize)
        resp = json.loads(client.do_action_with_exception(req).decode())
        return resp
```

## 错误
>   File "C:\Program Files\Python36\lib\site-packages\aliyunsdkcore\client.py", line 279, in do_action_with_exception
    request_id=request_id)
aliyunsdkcore.acs_exception.exceptions.ServerException: HTTP Status: 400 Error:RegionIdRequired x-acs-region-id is required. RequestID: None

其实已给出RegionId,但即使在add_query_params方法中单独指定,也是相同错误,提示无regionId,可若使用add_header(k='x-acs-region-id',v='cn-shenzhen')方法则通过.
Job deletion seems to be missing.

Originally on 2011-02-14

Uploading large files to bibupload presents a couple of problems which would be nice to fix:

1) bibupload large_file.xml

where large file is ~10^5 recs can kill the server by eating swap space.  bibupload could look at the xml file and chunk it before processing to prevent this type of occurrence.

2) bibupload small_file_??.xml

(i.e. breaking the large_file into several small ones and sending them in) will fail because bibupload will only take the first file (failing silently, I believe!)

bibupload should take all files given to it, and loop over them at a high level - i.e. running sequentially, not stitching it together as one file which would break the swapspace.

issue_title: Custom controller info may not be recorded in summary yaml
issue_body:
Controller modules may choose to allow customization of controller info during the test. E.g. `AndroidDevice.add_device_info` adds extra info of the controller objects to the summary yaml.

But `TestRunner` only record the controller info at the controller registration time, so the customized info may never get recorded depending on the implementation of the controller module.

I.e. If the info is a static string, we it will be set at registration time, and subsequent changes to the info field won't be picked up.

issue_title: Allow short flags, similar to Ansible
issue_body:
Our command line flags are too long and clunky. From the Ansible help menu, here are some examples of how Ansible core flags:

>   -f FORKS, --forks=FORKS
>   -e EXTRA_VARS, --extra-vars=EXTRA_VARS
>   -s, --sudo            run operations with sudo (nopasswd) (deprecated, use

tower-cli has these options or a simple option, but none of them have the short form. It has the `--forks` flag, but won't accept a `-f` flag.

For an example of allowing a shorthand flag, see `utils/decorators.py`. This shouldn't be hard to do, but we should go through all of the files in `resources/`, and use shorthand flags wherever they don't conflict with existing flags and would be helpful.

        if (ret == 0 and not self.fix_subdirectory_path_if_broken('pg_xlog' if get_major_version(self.data_dir) < 10.0 else 'pg_wal')):
            logger.debug('fix_subdirectory_path_if_broken returned False')
            return 2
        return ret
```

issue_title: Datastore Key's reference is missing location identifier in app argument in to_legacy_urlsafe()
issue_body:
@dhermes I put this as a new issue because I thought people might have a problem with this too. Please feel free to edit/remove this issue if it is not appropriate to put it here.

I was running `pip install --upgrade google-cloud-datastore` to get latest version that has the `key.to_legacy_urlsafe()` implemented in [#3491](https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/pull/3491). I tested the method and I found out that the urlsafe generated from the method is still not the same as datastore's urlsafe.

I have implemented my own function to generate the urlsafe key before the update was released and so far it worked properly for me (I can share my code if anybody wants). So I compared my function with the to_legacy_urlsafe and I found the problem:


```
reference = _app_engine_key_pb2.Reference(
        app=self.project,
        path=_to_legacy_path(self._path),  # Avoid the copy.
        name_space=self.namespace,
        )
```

In the app argument, the location identifier is missing unlike in my function where I concatenated the "s~" at the beginning of the string. I think this is the cause of the problem why it was not getting the urlsafe correct, although I don't understand why it needs the location identifier in front of the project name in the first place.

Do you think I should send a PR to fix this?

issue_title: Include fully qualified scope in build invalidator key
issue_body:
Because the `BuildInvalidator` directory is computed using a `Task.stable_name`, the same task installed in two different locations will use the same `BuildInvalidator` directory. This leads to errors where the `BuildInvalidator` can think that an earlier copy of a task running (which will put outputs in `.pants.d/${scope1}/**/*`) count as valid runs of the latter task (which will put outputs in `.pants.d/${scope2}/**/*`).

The location where a task is installed (its "`scope`") should also be included in the build invalidator location.

issue_title: JVM scopes not applied to strict_dependencies
issue_body:
`strict_dependencies` currently do not apply the `JvmTarget` `scope` argument, but should.

Since `strict_dependencies` are only used at compile time, this bug only affects compile time (ie, a `compile` scoped dep would properly be excluded at runtime).

Relates to #4874 ... we ought to do a wholesale rethinking of how both of these are computed (...or perhaps a reimplementation of `strict_dependencies` using `Target.closure` with the relevant dependency arguments?)

The `[info]` level messages (mostly) duplicate output from pants, and are impossible to tie back to compilation of a particular target anyway. We should almost never pass `info` to zinc... perhaps by always treating pants' `--level=info` flag as `warn` for zinc.

given a jvm_app target like so with a forward dependency edge to `src/java/com/twitter/typeahead:topic-backfill-builder-binary` by way of the `binary=` kwarg:

```
jvm_app(name='topic-backfill-builder-app',
  basename='topic-backfill-builder',
  binary = 'src/java/com/twitter/typeahead:topic-backfill-builder-binary',
  bundles = [
    bundle(fileset=rglobs('config/*', 'data/*', 'scripts/*')),
  ]
)
```

the v2 changed impl fails to detect this target when `src/java/com/twitter/typeahead:topic-backfill-builder-binary` falls in the transitive changed scope - while v1 changed does.

looks like a `traversable_dependency_specs` thing: https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/master/src/python/pants/backend/jvm/targets/jvm_app.py#L269

issue_title: Incompatibility with possible future regular expression syntax
issue_body:
Currently the `re` module supports only simple set syntax. But it is possible that in future it will support extended syntax: nested sets and set operations. Unfortunately that syntax is not fully compatible with the current syntax. In particular open bracket `'['` in a character set starts a nested set. The code of html5lib contains a regular expression that will be broken if the new syntax will be accepted.
```
ascii_punctuation_re = re.compile("[\u0009-\u000D\u0020-\u002F\u003A-\u0040\u005B-\u0060\u007B-\u007E]")
```
It would be good to guard the code from possible future breakage. It is enough to add a backslash before ``[``. Replace `\u005B` with `\u005C\u005B`, `\\\u005B` or `\\[`.

See Python issue: https://bugs.python.org/issue30349.

issue_title: File.filename doesn't apply when type is stream:
issue_body:
Setting a filename on an attachment only works when using `.data`. When `.stream` is used, it falls back to the name of the file on the uploading system.

```
        myfile = self.nylas_client.files.create()
        myfile.content_type = 'application/pdf'
        myfile.filename = attachment_name
        with open(attachment_path, 'rb') as f:
            myfile.stream = f
            myfile.save()

        myfile.filename = attachment_name
        # Create a new draft
        draft = self.nylas_client.drafts.create()
        if type(recipients) == str:
            recipients = [recipients]
        draft.to = [{'email': recipient} for recipient in recipients]
        draft.subject = subject
        draft.body = message
        draft.attach(myfile)
```

issue_title: Syncdb fails when there are non-app tables that the user doesn't have access to.
issue_body:
Syncdb runs this query:

SELECT TABLE_NAME FROM INFORMATION_SCHEMA.TABLES WHERE TABLE_TYPE = 'BASE TABLE'

to determine the list of tables to run this sort of query on:

ALTER TABLE [django_admin_log] NOCHECK CONSTRAINT ALL

If a different user, in a different schema, creates a table, say, [demo].[table_d3], syncdb will try to run this query:

ALTER TABLE [table_d3] NOCHECK CONSTRAINT ALL

which will raise this error:

Cannot find the object "table_d3" because it does not exist or you do not have permissions.

issue_title: Calling ReportService through a managed NAT resulting in closed connections
issue_body:
We have attempted to utilize both this and the Java lib to download DFP reports from the ReportService through a managed NAT service, whose default timeout is 350 seconds. The absence of a [Keep-Alive header](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Headers/Keep-Alive) on the http call forces the NAT to drop our connection prematurely.

https://github.com/googleads/googleads-python-lib/blob/master/googleads/common.py#L681 sets the socket timeout of the call to 1 hr, and this allows us to download the report from a public subnet, but our process must run behind the NAT and we're thus stymied with the current code base.

Is it possible to either a) add a Keep-Alive header with a similar timeout as in L681, or b) allow users to pass in the needed headers as part of the call? We'd hate to have to edit and keep synchronized changes to the library. 

Thanks!
Oskar

issue_title: example script pb_addNewServer.py has wrong default for DHCP
issue_body:
The example script `pb_addNewServer.py` adds a new server to an existing datacenter and connects it to a specified LAN.

The server's NIC is created with `dhcp=False` as default value.
This is in contrast to the standard of the API and SDK where the dafult value is `True`

The default value in this script must be the same as in API and SDK to avoid unexpected behaviour.

issue_title: PullImageTest pulls all tags; is slow with Docker < 1.13
issue_body:

        res = self.client.pull('hello-world')

> tag – Tag or digest. If empty when pulling an image, this causes all tags for the given image to be pulled.

```

When using docker.images.build with pull=True, credHelpers in ~/.docker/config.json are not passed. Thus the pull fails with `docker.errors.BuildError: unauthorized: You don't have the needed permissions to perform this operation, and you may have invalid credentials.`.

it seems that `docker.api.build.BuildApiMixin.build` shoud use
```
header = auth.get_config_header(self, registry)
if header:
    headers['X-Registry-Auth'] = header
```
instead of its own implementation (`docker.api.build.BuildApiMixin._set_auth_headers`)


docker config:

While trying to create a publishing only loop on a separate thread to push progress reports on a process, I ran into trouble trying to run the loop on a separate thread. 

`ValueError: set_wakeup_fd only works in main thread`

The problem comes from the ApplicationRunner trying to add a signal handler on the loop in the thread, which in turn triggers a test verifying that the signal is not handled anywhere but in the main thread.

```python
try:
    # set_wakeup_fd() raises ValueError if this is not the
    # main thread.  By calling it early we ensure that an
    # event loop running in another thread cannot add a signal
    # handler.
    signal.set_wakeup_fd(self._csock.fileno())
except (ValueError, OSError) as exc:
    raise RuntimeError(str(exc))
```

It would be nice to be able to be able to:

1.  toggle the handling of the SIGTERM by the runner using an argument,
2.  close the runner using a `stop()` or `close()` method

https://github.com/allenai/ai2thor/blob/master/ai2thor/controller.py#L957 
`raise_for_failure` should be an argument of `self.step()`, not `dict()`

issue_title: growth strategy for unknown parameter types does not work with EXASOL
issue_body:
If you execute a parametrised query where EXASOL does not know exactly the type, like:
`cursor.execute("SELECT x from (values(1), (2)) as tbl (x) where x = ?;" , [2,])`
turbodbc allocates a huge amount of memory. This combined with the default value for parameter_sets_to_buffer could lead to a MemoryError.

### What is wrong?

The `evm.utils.numeric` module could be cleaned up.


### How can it be fixed

- switch to use `eth_utils.int_to_big_endian` and `eth_utils.big_endian_to_int`
- change `int_to_bytes32` to use `value.to_bytes(32, 'big')` but preserve the validation checks.
- remove `int_to_byte` as it doesn't appear to be used anywhere.
- remove `byte_to_int` as it isn't used.
- remove `safe_ord` as it should not be needed anymore.

issue_title: `conda info -a` suggests `conda install -n root _license` instead of `... -n base ...`
issue_body:
<!--
Hi!  Read this; it's important.

This is an issue tracker for conda -- the package manager.  File feature requests
for conda here, as well as bug reports about something conda has messed up.

If your issue is a bug report or feature request for:
  * a specific conda package from Anaconda ('defaults' channel):
      ==> file at https://github.com/ContinuumIO/anaconda-issues
  * a specific conda package from conda-forge:
      ==> file at the corresponding feedstock under https://github.com/conda-forge
  * repo.anaconda.com access and service:
      ==> file at https://github.com/ContinuumIO/anaconda-issues
  * anaconda.org access and service:
      ==> file at https://anaconda.org/contact/report
  * commands under 'conda build':
      ==> file at https://github.com/conda/conda-build
  * commands under 'conda env':
      ==> please file it here!
  * all other conda commands that start with 'conda':
      ==> please file it here!

If you continue on, and especially if you are submitting a bug report,

   **please include all requested information below.**

If a maintainer determines the information is required to understand 
your issue, and if it is not provided, your issue may be
closed automatically.

-->

**I'm submitting a...**
  - [X] bug report
  - [ ] feature request


## Current Behavior
<!-- What actually happens?
     If you want to include console output, please use "Steps to Reproduce" below. -->

### Steps to Reproduce
<!-- If the current behavior is a bug, please provide specific, minimal steps to independently reproduce.
     Include the exact conda commands that reproduce the issue and their output between the ticks below. -->
`conda info -a` shows the following at the end:
```
WARNING: could not import _license.show_info
# try:
# $ conda install -n root _license
```


## Expected Behavior
<!-- What do you think should happen? -->
`root` has been renamed to `base` so it should say `conda install -n base _license`.

issue_title: OSX: Error: `Error:  Package missing in current osx-64 channels:    - package_name`
issue_body:
After installing Anaconda (both for python 2 and 3), when I run `conda create` the following error is displayed:
`Error:  Package missing in current osx-64 channels:    - package_name`

I realised the file `.condarc` had `PACKAGE_NAME` included which caused the error. I removed manually and solved the issue.

(OS X 10.10)

issue_title: pip breaks conda environments by changing content of existing inodes
issue_body:
Under the most common mode of operation, conda makes extensive use of hard links to optimize speed and disk space efficiency.  Rather than make a copy of every file being installed into every environment, we extract a package into a writable package cache, and then use hard links to install most all paths into a conda environment.  That is, each path for a package will be hard-linked into an environment, and we have only one inode for two paths on the file system.

Any further use of the same package in other conda environments will also result in hard links from the package cache.  Thus if you have three environments all using the same package (plus the existence of the extracted package in the package cache), for each file in the package, you have four paths sharing the same inode, and you are only using 1/4 the disk space you normally would if you created full copies of each file.

When pip installs a package into a conda environment, it will blissfully clobber paths that conda has already installed files to.  Worse than the clobbering though, pip does not sever the path/inode connection before clobbering, and thus replaces content in the existing inode (rather than creating a new one).  When that happens, pip modifies not only the current environment, but *all* environments on the system that have that package installed into it.

issue_title: conda search behaves inconsistently for packages which don't exist at all vs packages for which the specific version doen't exist
issue_body:
Testing on conda 4.3, roughly

On macOS, neither 'r-vars=1.5_2=r3.3.1_*' nor 'r-lattice=0.20_34=r3.3.1_*' exist (though other versions of `r-lattice` do):

```
$ conda search --override-channels -c r --spec 'r-lattice=0.20_34=r3.3.1_*'
Fetching package metadata ...........


PackageNotFoundError: Package not found: '' Package missing in current osx-64 channels: 
  - r-lattice 0.20_34* r3.3.1_*

$ echo $?
1
```

```
$ conda search --override-channels -c r --spec 'r-vars=1.5_2=r3.3.1_*'
Fetching package metadata ...........

$ echo $?
0
```

I am currently working around this by checking the number of lines in stdout which is not ideal.

issue_title: Cannot install microscopes-common due to conflict
issue_body:
I am attempting to install microscopes-common from the channel datamicroscopes but have discovered a conflict between bottleneck and microscopes-common based on different dependencies on numpy.  While attempting the installation, I receive the following message:

$ conda install microscopes-common

UnsatisfiableError: The following specifications were found to be in conflict:
  - bottleneck
  - microscopes-common
Use "conda info <package>" to see the dependencies for each package.

Attached are the conda list and dependencies for bottleneck and microscopes-common.

[conda_list.txt](https://github.com/conda/conda/files/1192370/conda_list.txt)
[bottleneck_info.txt](https://github.com/conda/conda/files/1192371/bottleneck_info.txt)
[microscopes-common.txt](https://github.com/conda/conda/files/1192373/microscopes-common.txt)


My installation of anaconda2 on Linux 64-bit was downloaded from: https://www.continuum.io/downloads

It seems that the microscopes-common package depends on numpy 1.8.2, but there does not seem to be a bottleneck build that depends on numpy 1.8.2.

Is there anyway to work around this problem?

issue_title: conda env create -f hangs if yaml is on read-only network share
issue_body:
**I'm submitting a...** - [x] bug report - [ ] feature request ## Current Behavior I'm having a problem where `conda env create -f ...` hangs when the provided yaml file is on a read-only Windows share. ### Steps to Reproduce Put any environment.yaml containing pip packages on a read-only Windows SMB share. Attempt to create the environment by 1. `conda env create -f \\server\ReadOnlyShare\environment.yaml` 2. `conda env create -f M:\environment.yaml` where the read-only share is mounted at M:\ 3. `pushd \\server\ReadOnlyShare` then `conda env create -f environment.yaml` In particular, it hangs even when I call `conda env create` from within a writable local directory. Calling with `-v` indicates it is trying to create a temporary file in the unwritable directory right at the point it hangs ``` conda env create -f -v \\server\ReadOnlyShare\Python\envs\testenv.yaml [...] ===> LINKING PACKAGE: defaults::qt-5.6.2-vc14_6 <=== prefix=C:\Anaconda3\envs\testenv source=C:\Anaconda3\pkgs\qt-5.6.2-vc14_6 ===> LINKING PACKAGE: defaults::pyqt-5.6.0-py36_2 <=== prefix=C:\Anaconda3\envs\testenv source=C:\Anaconda3\pkgs\pyqt-5.6.0-py36_2 ===> LINKING PACKAGE: defaults::matplotlib-2.0.2-np113py36_0 <=== prefix=C:\Anaconda3\envs\testenv source=C:\Anaconda3\pkgs\matplotlib-2.0.2-np113py36_0 done [HANGS HERE UNTIL CTRL-C] Traceback (most recent call last): File "C:\Anaconda3\lib\tempfile.py", line 260, in _mkstemp_inner fd = _os.open(file, flags, 0o600) PermissionError: [Errno 13] Permission denied: 'M:\\condaenv.u72ohzul.requirements.txt' During handling of the above exception, another exception occurred: Traceback (most recent call last): File "C:\Anaconda3\lib\site-packages\conda\exceptions.py", line 789, in __call__ return func(*args, **kwargs) File "C:\Anaconda3\lib\site-packages\conda_env\cli\main.py", line 74, in do_call exit_code = getattr(module, func_name)(args, parser) File "C:\Anaconda3\lib\site-packages\conda_env\cli\main_create.py", line 108, in execute installer.install(prefix, pkg_specs, args, env) File "C:\Anaconda3\lib\site-packages\conda_env\installers\pip.py", line 36, in _pip_install_via_requirements delete=False) File "C:\Anaconda3\lib\tempfile.py", line 549, in NamedTemporaryFile (fd, name) = _mkstemp_inner(dir, prefix, suffix, flags, output_type) File "C:\Anaconda3\lib\tempfile.py", line 260, in _mkstemp_inner fd = _os.open(file, flags, 0o600) KeyboardInterrupt ``` If I copy the yaml to a local drive, `conda env create` works as expected.
